sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Apple honors 2024 App Store Award winners
feed.summary: Apple announced the winners of the 2024 App Store Awards, recognizing 17 apps and games for their exceptional user experiences, design, and innovation. The awards include categories for various Apple devices, the inaugural Apple Vision Pro category, and Cultural Impact winners, highlighting developers who have enriched users' lives and communities. The winning selections showcase the diverse opportunities available across the App Store and the Apple ecosystem.

feed.title: Should You Invest in the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC)?
feed.summary: This article evaluates the Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC) as an investment option for broad exposure to the Technology - Broad sector. It highlights FTEC's passive management, low expense ratio (0.08%), and strong year-to-date performance (up 31.10%). The piece also details its top holdings, risk profile, and compares it with alternative technology ETFs like XLK and VGT, ultimately giving FTEC a "Strong Buy" Zacks ETF Rank.

feed.title: 3 Vanguard ETFs That Can Help Build Your Perfect Portfolio
feed.summary: This article highlights three Vanguard ETFs—the S&P 500 ETF (VOO), Growth ETF (VUG), and Total Bond Market ETF (BND)—as key components for building a balanced, long-term investment portfolio. It emphasizes Vanguard's low-cost structure and passively managed funds as an effective wealth-building strategy. The article provides insights into each ETF's performance, holdings, and expense ratios, along with a revised framework for age-based asset allocation.

feed.title: Fatal mistake that turned Kodak from a $31,000,000,000 photography company to bankrupt
feed.summary: This article details the downfall of Kodak, once a $31 billion photography giant, that ultimately led to its bankruptcy. The company's fatal error was its reluctance to embrace digital photography, despite an employee inventing the first handheld digital camera in 1975, because it prioritized profitable film sales. Although Kodak later tried to enter the digital market, it was too late and faced intense competition, low profit margins, and financial struggles, eventually filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2012.

feed.title: Match Group Warns Negative Currency Impacts Will Bring Down Revenue
feed.summary: Match Group announced that unfavorable currency exchange rates will cause its current-quarter revenue to be below earlier estimates, reducing fourth-quarter revenue by $15 million more than anticipated, primarily impacting Tinder. The company also noted that Tinder's new user declines on Apple iOS have not fully recovered, though it did announce an additional $1.5 billion in stock buybacks. Match Group shares fell over 5% on the news and are down about 14% year-to-date.

feed.title: GROZ Top Holdings List & Exposure (Zacks Focus Growth ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings of the Zacks Focus Growth ETF (GROZ), which primarily invests in large-cap North American companies. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, and Alphabet, with technology making up the largest sector exposure. The article also breaks down the ETF's geographic, currency, and industry exposures, showing a strong concentration in the United States and the technology sector.

feed.title: Report: Apple working with Broadcom to develop custom chipset for AI
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly collaborating with Broadcom Inc. to develop a custom server processor, codenamed “Baltra,” for AI features in its iOS operating system, with a targeted launch in 2026. This move aligns with Apple's strategy of using proprietary chips for on-device AI and Broadcom's expertise in semiconductor interconnect designs. The project likely leverages Broadcom's advanced packaging technologies for faster chip-to-chip communications.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 61% of the company
feed.summary: Institutional investors hold 61% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), making them the most powerful shareholder group with significant influence on the company's share price. The Vanguard Group, Inc. is the largest shareholder with 8.9% of shares, while insiders own less than 1% and the general public holds a 37% stake.

feed.title: 6 Best Ways to Redeem Aeroplan Points
feed.summary: This article by NerdWallet Canada outlines six effective strategies for maximizing the value of Aeroplan points. Key recommendations include understanding the average point value, considering partner airlines for better redemption rates, opting for business-class flights, identifying "sweet spot" routes, utilizing family sharing plans, and prioritizing flight redemptions over other options like gift cards or car rentals. The article emphasizes calculating the cents-per-point value to ensure worthwhile redemptions.

feed.title: Broadcom ETFs Surge on Strong AI Demand
feed.summary: Broadcom's shares surged by 20% following strong earnings and optimistic projections for its AI business, pushing its market cap past $1 trillion. This surge significantly boosted ETFs with large Broadcom holdings, such as the Defiance Daily Target 2X Long AVGO ETF (AVGX) and the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX). The demand for Broadcom's custom AI chips, favored by major tech companies like Alphabet, Meta, and Apple, is seen as a key driver for its continued growth.

feed.title: Sonos Arc Ultra review: The best Dolby Atmos soundbar just got better
feed.summary: The Sonos Arc Ultra soundbar aims to redeem the brand after a problematic app rollout. It significantly upgrades over its predecessor with a 9.1.4-channel configuration, Sound Motion technology for improved audio quality in a more compact design, and Bluetooth support. While a substantial upgrade for new buyers seeking premium home theater sound, current Sonos Arc owners may not find the improvements significant enough to warrant replacement.

feed.title: TSMC says first advanced U.S. chip plant 'dang near back' on schedule. Here's an inside look at the Arizona fab
feed.summary: TSMC's first advanced U.S. chip fabrication plant in Arizona is "dang near back on the original schedule" after delays and an increased investment from $12 billion to $20 billion. The plant will produce 4-nanometer chips and has committed to building two more fabs by the end of the decade, totaling $65 billion. Challenges include securing a skilled workforce, managing significant water and power demands, and global supply chain complexities, but the U.S. Commerce Department finalized $6.6 billion in CHIPS Act funding for the project.

feed.title: Liberty All-Star Growth Fund Delivers Stellar 8.7% Monthly Return, YTD Gains Exceed 20% with Tech-Heavy Portfolio
feed.summary: The Liberty All-Star Growth Fund (ASG) reported robust performance for November 2024, with its Net Asset Value (NAV) increasing by 8.72% and its market price by 7.73%. Year-to-date, the fund's NAV rose by 20.25% and its market price by 21.05%. The fund maintains a technology-heavy portfolio, with top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, and Amazon, and primarily invests in Information Technology.

feed.title: Broadcom's AI Chip Leadership Puts Pressure on Nvidia and Marvell, Analysts Say
feed.summary: Broadcom's stock surged after reporting strong Q4 adjusted EPS results and raising its dividend, driven by its leadership in AI custom Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs). Analysts upgraded Broadcom's price targets, highlighting its expanding AI opportunities and advanced 3nm silicon technology, which puts it ahead of competitors like Nvidia and Marvell. The company anticipates significant growth in its AI revenues, expecting to reach $30 billion by calendar 2027 based on strong demand from hyperscalers.

feed.title: Will the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) return another 36% in 2025?
feed.summary: The article discusses the extraordinary 36.34% year-to-date return of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) in 2024, largely driven by the "magnificent seven" big tech stocks. It explores whether this strong performance is sustainable for 2025, referencing an expert's bullish outlook on US equities due to anticipated earnings growth, healthy economic projections, and falling inflation. The expert suggests that the high valuations of the big tech companies are justified by strong fundamentals.

feed.title: 11%+ Yields From AI Stocks: QQQI Vs. QYLD (NASDAQ:QQQI)
feed.summary: This article compares QQQI and QYLD, two funds designed to generate high yields from mega-cap AI companies, to help income-focused investors choose a suitable diversification instrument. While mega-cap tech stocks typically offer low dividends, these funds aim to provide attractive income opportunities. The analysis details their characteristics and suitability for an income portfolio, recommending that interested investors can access further insights through High Yield Investor's service.

feed.title: A new investment fund is saying ‘no’ to DEI-focused companies — and ‘yes’ to others
feed.summary: Azoria, a new investment firm, is launching an ETF that will exclude S&P 500 companies with explicit racial and gender hiring targets. The firm believes companies hiring based on skill and ability will financially outperform those focused on DEI, citing examples like Best Buy and the underperformance of DEI-focused companies. Azoria aims to provide investors with a meritocracy-rooted alternative, investing in companies that abandon such hiring practices.

feed.title: Invesco launches ETF to maximize on the tech concentration craze
feed.summary: Invesco has launched a new exchange-traded fund, the Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG), designed to provide investors with exposure to the top 45% of companies within the Nasdaq-100 Index. This move by Invesco, known for its large Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), aims to meet investor demand for capturing the megacap concentration story within the Nasdaq, with initial top holdings including Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. The launch reflects a broader trend of new funds emerging to address market enthusiasm for or aversion to megacap exposure.

feed.title: Charted: Magnificent 7 Market Cap as a Share of the S&P 500
feed.summary: This article examines the increasing share of the S&P 500's market capitalization held by the "Magnificent 7" stocks over the past decade. It highlights that these seven stocks have grown by nearly 800% in market cap, reaching almost 35% of the S&P 500 by mid-2024, raising concerns about potential over-exposure for investors.

feed.title: Victoria’s Secret Is PETA’s ‘Company of the Year’ for its Fabulously Innovative Feather-Free Fashion Show
feed.summary: PETA has named Victoria’s Secret its 2024 Company of the Year for its innovative use of feather-free materials in its fashion show, showcasing vegan Angel wings made from 3-D printed materials, quilted satin, soda can tabs, and Swarovski crystals. This decision followed PETA's communication with the retailer about the cruelty involved in obtaining feathers from birds. The move is applauded by PETA as an example for designers and consumers to adopt animal-friendly practices in fashion.

feed.title: Pattern Energy Announces Equity Investment from Consortium Headed by APG and ART
feed.summary: Pattern Energy announced that a consortium led by APG Asset Management and Australian Retirement Trust will acquire Riverstone Holdings LLC’s equity stake in the company. This investment strengthens Pattern Energy's position, allowing it to advance its development pipeline of over 25-gigawatts of renewable energy and transmission projects. The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2025.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE: Leveraged ETFs Offer Short-Term Edge As Traders Eye Semiconductors, China, And Quantum Tech In Post-Election Market
feed.summary: Traders are increasingly leveraging ETFs to capitalize on short-term market opportunities, especially after recent elections. Analysts from Direxion and The Mercator LLC highlighted strategies in semiconductors due to AI and quantum computing advancements, and in Chinese markets following stimulus measures. Single-stock ETFs are also gaining popularity for tactical trading in high-profile companies, though experts caution on understanding their short-term nature and daily rebalancing effects.

feed.title: ABFL Top Holdings List & Exposure (Abacus FCF Leaders ETF)
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings for the Abacus FCF Leaders ETF (ABFL), including company names, weights, and shares held. It also breaks down ABFL's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures. The ETF's top holdings are NVIDIA, Apple, and Mastercard, with its largest exposure to the United States and the Technology sector.

feed.title: Apple says it has told suppliers to stop buying minerals from Congo
feed.summary: Apple has instructed its suppliers to halt the purchase of tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold from the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda due to escalating conflict in the region. This decision comes in response to allegations from the Congolese government that Apple uses conflict minerals laundered through international supply chains. Apple strongly disputes these allegations.

feed.title: Your next TV won’t be micro-LED. Here’s why
feed.summary: The article explains why micro-LED TVs, despite their stunning visual quality, are unlikely to become mainstream consumer technology soon. The primary challenges are the extreme difficulty and cost associated with manufacturing them at smaller, consumer-friendly sizes, mainly due to pixel pitch and heat management issues. The author suggests that electroluminescent quantum dots (QDEL) and quantum dot micro-LEDs are more promising future display technologies, while PHOLED offers an evolutionary, rather than revolutionary, advancement.

feed.title: Apple Supplier Jabil Soars On Q1 Earnings Beat Fueled By Data Center Demand: Retail Gets Bullish
feed.summary: Jabil Inc., an Apple supplier, saw its shares jump by 10% after reporting first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations. The strong performance was driven by robust demand in its intelligent infrastructure segment, particularly in cloud, data center, and warehouse automation markets, leading to an "extremely bullish" retail sentiment. Despite an overall revenue decline due to a business transition, the company provided an optimistic outlook for Q2 2025 and the full fiscal year.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is a favorite amongst institutional investors who own 61%
feed.summary: Institutional investors own 61% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), making it a favored stock among this group. While this signifies credibility, the concentration of ownership among a few large investors means their trading actions can significantly impact the stock price. Insiders, despite owning less than 1%, hold a substantial dollar value in shares and have been recent sellers.

feed.title: After Earnings, Is Broadcom Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
feed.summary: Broadcom's third-quarter earnings report, released on December 12, 2024, has led Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate for the company's stock to $190 per share, up from $155, driven by an increased AI revenue growth forecast. Despite the upward revision, Morningstar now considers Broadcom's shares to be roughly fairly valued after a significant post-earnings jump, giving it a 2-star rating. The article highlights Broadcom's strong potential in AI chips, its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs, and its financial strength, while also noting risks related to market cycles and supply chain reliance on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

feed.title: JEPQ vs. QQQ: Which ETF Aligns With Your Investment Goals?
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, JEPQ and QQQ, both tied to the Nasdaq 100, highlighting their distinct investment strategies. JEPQ focuses on income generation and reduced volatility through covered call strategies, offering a 9.2% annual yield, while QQQ provides direct exposure to the Nasdaq 100 with a focus on growth and higher volatility. The analysis delves into their historical performance, risk metrics, income stability, and suitability for different investment goals, concluding that JEPQ is better for income-seekers and QQQ for growth-oriented investors.

feed.title: Jabil’s Winning Streak Begins: Market Bottom Set, Future in Focus
feed.summary: Jabil's fiscal Q1 2025 report indicates that the company has hit its market bottom, with a strong outlook for future growth despite a top-line contraction. The company outperformed expectations for both revenue and earnings, providing optimistic guidance and benefiting from strength in cloud, data center, and digital commerce segments. Analyst and institutional investor trends are positive, supporting a potential new all-time high for the stock.

feed.title: ‘Synaptics plans to assemble 10% chips in India’
feed.summary: US semiconductor company Synaptics Inc. plans to assemble and package 10% of its chips in India by 2027 to diversify its supply chain and reduce reliance on China. The company will use an OSAT facility in Hyderabad and partner with embed UR to develop edge AI solutions, aiming for the process to begin in late 2025 or early 2026. This move also addresses concerns about proposed US tariffs and the need for supply chain alternatives.

feed.title: Analyst says Celestica’s momentum has ‘continued’ through the year
feed.summary: Robert Young, managing director at Canada Canaccord Genuity, highlights Celestica's strong performance, attributing it to conservative guidance and a "beat and raise cadence." The company's significant business with top hyper scalers, including Google and Meta, for designing networking gear and AI servers, is a major growth driver. Young notes that Celestica is increasingly recognized for its higher-value OEM role due to its IP, moving beyond its traditional EMS vendor perception.

feed.title: The 'boring' department store whose stock is outperforming Tesla
feed.summary: Dillard's, a regional department store, has seen its stock outperform Tesla, Apple, and Microsoft over the last five years, with a 500% increase. This success is attributed to its "drama-free consistency," focus on business fundamentals, supplier and employee loyalty, and a strategy of avoiding constant discounting. Dillard's has also been surprisingly tech-focused, adopting real-time sales and inventory tracking earlier than rivals, leading activist investor Barington Capital to suggest Macy's emulate its model.

feed.title: Corsair K65 Plus Apple Edition Review: Just Buy the Non-Apple Version
feed.summary: The Corsair K65 Plus Apple Edition keyboard is functionally identical to the standard version but costs more due to its Apple-exclusive colorways. While it offers excellent pre-lubed linear switches ideal for gaming and good build quality, its custom RGB lighting and key remapping features are limited in wireless modes. The reviewer recommends opting for the standard black version as it frequently goes on sale and provides the same performance without the inflated price of the Apple-exclusive model.

feed.title: Apple looks to make a splash in 2025 with AI-infused smart home devices
feed.summary: Apple is set to launch an AI-powered smart home hub in 2025 with a 6-inch display and new operating system, alongside upgrades to existing Home devices. The company is also developing an in-home security camera and a smart doorbell with advanced facial recognition, leveraging its commitment to privacy to gain consumer trust and expand revenue in the smart home market. These new offerings signal a significant push by Apple into the connected home ecosystem.

feed.title: Apple to see strong Christmas iPhone sales that launch a ‘renaissance of growth’
feed.summary: Apple is projected to experience a renaissance of growth driven by strong iPhone sales during the holiday season, fueled by an estimated 300 million iPhones over four years old. This potential "supercycle" is expected to lead to significant upgrades to new AI-capable iPhones, especially in the key China market, and could propel Apple to a $4 trillion market cap.

feed.title: Apple approaches $4 trillion valuation as investors bet on AI momentum
feed.summary: Apple is nearing a $4 trillion market valuation, driven by investor enthusiasm for its AI enhancements, which are expected to boost iPhone sales. The company has outpaced Nvidia and Microsoft in this race, with a roughly 16% share jump since November adding about $500 billion to its market cap. Analysts anticipate a rebound in iPhone revenue in 2025 as Apple integrates generative AI technology across its devices.

feed.title: Globalstar director James Monroe III buys $985k in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Globalstar director James Monroe III has purchased 500,000 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $985,050. This acquisition comes as Globalstar shows strong momentum with an 87.5% price return over the past six months and reported record Q3 revenue, alongside strategic advancements in 5G technology and satellite connectivity with Apple. The company has also announced plans for a reverse stock split to optimize its capital structure.

feed.title: Evolving the device experience at Microsoft
feed.summary: Microsoft is transitioning its employee device management from a traditional on-premises model to a modern, cloud-based approach using Microsoft Intune, Azure Active Directory, and co-management strategies. This shift supports hybrid work, enables employees to be productive from anywhere on various devices (Windows, Android, iOS, macOS), and enhances security through Zero Trust principles and verified device health. The migration addresses challenges by using Hybrid Azure AD joined devices as a bridge and standardizing management practices.

feed.title: Apple’s been on an uncommonly strong run. So a tough January could now be in the cards.
feed.summary: Apple's stock has achieved a rare five-week streak of 2%-plus gains, a phenomenon that historically precedes a roughly 5% drop in the following month. Investors are advised to be cautious as this pattern suggests a potentially tough January for Apple shares. The stock's recent performance marks the first time in 14 years and very rarely in the company's 44-year public history that it has had such a strong, sustained run.

feed.title: Why Samsung and LG may be the reason Apple delaying the all-screen iPhone
feed.summary: Apple's plan for an all-screen iPhone has been delayed past 2026, reportedly due to technical challenges faced by display manufacturers Samsung Display and LG Display. They are struggling with developing thin film encapsulation for OLEDs and suitable optical clear adhesives, pushing the potential release to 2027 or later. Apple aims for a bezel-free design that avoids the "magnifying glass effect" seen in other curved-screen phones.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Apple Inc. (AAPL): ‘It Was The Dumb Money That Was Getting Out, Because Warren Buffett Or One Of His Assistants Made Us Nervous’
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) within the context of AI stocks and overall market performance in 2024. He highlighted that "dumb money" sold off Apple shares due to investor nervousness, potentially influenced by Warren Buffett, despite strong underlying sales. Cramer also offered trading advice for 2025, emphasizing the importance of not getting intoxicated by gains, having the fortitude to hold stocks during tough times, and carefully staging buy orders.

feed.title: Growth ETFs Shaken Up as Tech Takes on Value Blush
feed.summary: Growth ETFs like IVW are undergoing significant rebalancing as major tech companies such as Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Adobe are no longer considered pure growth stocks by S&P Global. This reclassification means investors will see a smaller allocation to these tech giants in growth ETFs and a larger presence of financial firms like Berkshire Hathaway and JPMorgan Chase, which are now being given a "growth blush". The rebalancing also shifts tech's share in the S&P Value Index significantly, with these companies now having their weightings divided between growth and value characteristics based on S&P's multifactor analysis.

feed.title: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) Poised for Strong 2025 Growth with Custom AI Chips and Strategic Partnerships, Says William Blair
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is anticipated to experience strong growth in 2025, driven by its custom AI chip development and strategic partnerships with major cloud customers, according to William Blair. The company recently surpassed a $1 trillion market cap and its CEO announced the development of custom AI chips with three large cloud clients. William Blair maintains an Outperform rating, expecting steady growth from existing partnerships and potential new clients like ByteDance, OpenAI, and Apple.

feed.title: Large-Cap Tech Stocks Could Soar Again in 2025
feed.summary: Large-cap tech stocks, particularly the Magnificent Seven, are expected to continue their dominance and strong performance in 2025, according to market observers. Despite concerns over valuations and regulation, their focus on innovation and diversification positions them for another banner year. ETFs like Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) offer efficient exposure to these high-performing companies for growth-focused portfolios.

feed.title: Corsair Gaming Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
feed.summary: Corsair Gaming announced its financial results for Q4 and full year 2024, reporting solid Q4 revenue and adjusted EBITDA driven by strong growth in the Gamer and Creator Peripherals segment. The company provided its 2025 outlook, anticipating revenue between $1.4 billion and $1.6 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $80 million to $100 million, expecting growth due to new high-performance GPU cards and strategic acquisitions like Fanatec.

feed.title: Apple class action allege App Store offers malicious crypto apps
feed.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Inc., claiming the company authorized and maintained malicious crypto applications on its App Store, leading to the theft of consumers' personal financial assets. Plaintiffs allege Apple misrepresented the safety and legality of these apps, including Digicoins, SolLuna, and Forex5, which they assert were "spoofing" programs designed to steal account information. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for alleged violations of California's Consumers Legal Remedies Act and Unfair Competition Law.

feed.title: Experts Think a Broadcom Stock Split Is Coming in 2025. Should You Buy AVGO Now?
feed.summary: Experts anticipate another Broadcom (AVGO) stock split in 2025 following a significant rally in 2024 driven by its AI and semiconductor performance. Broadcom's strong fundamentals, including impressive revenue and earnings growth, high free cash flow, and strategic advancements in AI and edge computing, reinforce its appeal. Analysts maintain a "Strong Buy" rating for AVGO stock, signaling confidence in its continued growth trajectory.

feed.title: Li Lu – Top 10 Holdings – Latest 13F
feed.summary: This article details the top 10 holdings of superinvestor Li Lu's portfolio as of June 30, 2024, based on his latest 13F-HR filing. His portfolio has a market value of over $2.5 billion, with significant concentrations in companies like Bank of America, Alphabet, Berkshire Hathaway, and Apple. The article emphasizes using 13F-HR documents to track superinvestors' strategies.

feed.title: Surgical Trades on the Magnificent 7
feed.summary: This article discusses Direxion's leveraged and inverse ETFs designed for trading the Magnificent 7 stocks, either individually or as a collective basket. It highlights how these products allow traders to seek profit or hedge risk based on market direction and news, while also outlining the significant risks associated with single stock and leveraged ETFs, emphasizing they are for sophisticated and aggressive investors.

feed.title: Apple Intelligence
feed.summary: Apple Intelligence integrates generative AI into iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Apple Vision Pro, focusing on enhancing communication, image creation, and productivity. Key features include Writing Tools for text refinement, Live Translation, Image Playground for generating images and Genmoji, and an enhanced Siri with on-screen awareness and device knowledge, all while prioritizing user privacy through on-device processing and Private Cloud Compute. The system also seamlessly integrates ChatGPT for broader expertise.

feed.title: Apple Watch For Your Kids
feed.summary: This article highlights the features and benefits of Apple Watch for kids, emphasizing how parents can manage their children's watches using their own iPhone. Key functionalities include communication, location tracking, Schooltime mode, health and activity features, emergency SOS, and access to Apple Music and the App Store. The Apple Watch is presented as a tool for independence for children and peace of mind for parents.

feed.title: Buy Apple Vision Pro
feed.summary: This page is a product listing for the Apple Vision Pro, detailing available purchasing options and extensive terms and conditions for financing through Apple Card Monthly Installments (ACMI) and AppleCare plans. It outlines eligibility, APR details, and coverage specifics for various Apple services and products related to the Vision Pro.

feed.title: When $2B facility opens, Amkor will become Peoria's largest private employer
feed.summary: Amkor Technology Inc.'s planned $2 billion semiconductor packaging and testing plant in Peoria, recently greenlit by the City Council, is poised to become the city's largest private employer. This mega-project will bring 2,000 new jobs to the Phoenix metro area and significantly bolster the U.S. semiconductor supply chain, with the facility packaging and testing chips for major companies like TSMC and Apple. Amkor, a leading provider in outsourced semiconductor services, had $6.5 billion in annual sales in 2023 and employs 30,000 worldwide.

feed.title: Amazon’s Prime Big Deal Days was the Company’s Biggest October Shopping Event Ever
feed.summary: Amazon's Prime Big Deal Days was its biggest October shopping event ever, with more Prime members participating and saving over $1 billion globally on various products. The event marked a strong start to the holiday shopping season, featuring innovative shopping tools like Rufus and Inspire, and Amazon plans further deals through the end of the year, including specific holiday events and the NFL Black Friday Football game.

feed.title: When does Amazon Prime Day 2024 end? How to score last minute deals before it's too late
feed.summary: Amazon Prime Day 2024 is concluding in the early hours of Wednesday and Thursday, depending on the time zone, giving shoppers a final chance to snag deals on various products. The two-day event, which started Tuesday, features discounts on Amazon services and devices, as well as popular items like Apple AirPods, iRobot Roomba, and Renpho Eye Massagers. The sales are exclusive to Prime members, though non-members can sign up for a free 30-day trial to participate.

feed.title: Amazon’s AI spending plans keep stock from joining tech rebound
feed.summary: Amazon's shares are lagging behind its megacap peers due to its substantial spending on artificial intelligence computing, which is impacting its profits. Investors are concerned that increased capital spending will negatively affect cash flows, especially as the company has historically performed better when focused on profitability. This shift to investment mode, coupled with Amazon's lack of a dividend and less aggressive share buyback program compared to competitors, is causing investors to pause on the stock.

feed.title: How Boston Scientific's digital and IT boss upgraded her team from order takers to strategic thinkers
feed.summary: Jodi Eddy, Boston Scientific's SVP and chief information and digital officer, transformed her IT team from order-takers to strategic thinkers, enabling technology to address complex healthcare systems. Eddy implemented a digital leader on every executive management board and oversaw the development of a new e-commerce site. The article also discusses Boston Scientific's cautious exploration of generative AI for internal use, while highlighting its focus on supporting human decision-making rather than replacing it.

feed.title: Ford most at risk from China exposure among 250 American companies, consulting firm says
feed.summary: A consulting firm, Strategy Risks, has identified Ford Motor Co. as the American company most exposed to risks from China among 250 of the largest listed U.S. companies. Ford scored 69 out of 100 on their exposure index, surpassing Apple and Tesla, due to factors like revenue in China, partnerships, and supply chain risks. Ford declined to comment on the rankings, citing a lack of detailed methodology.

feed.title: Cirrus Logic Celebrates 40 Years of Innovation and Excellence
feed.summary: Cirrus Logic, a leader in mixed-signal processing, is celebrating its 40th anniversary, reflecting on its journey from a startup to a global technology innovator. The company has a rich history of developing groundbreaking solutions for audio and electronics industries, holding over 3,900 patents, and has grown through strategic refocuses and acquisitions like Wolfson Microelectronics. CEO John Forsyth and VP Carl Alberty emphasize the company's commitment to innovation, customer trust, and its award-winning culture that fosters employee loyalty and professional growth.

feed.title: How Corning is ushering in the AI revolution
feed.summary: Corning, a 173-year-old glass-making company, is playing a crucial role in the AI revolution by producing next-generation fiber optic cables that power data centers for tech giants. Under CEO Wendell Weeks, the company has seen its market cap reach $41 billion and 2023 revenue hit $13 billion, with optical fiber now contributing 30% of its revenue. Weeks' leadership and innovative approach, admired by figures like Jeff Bezos and Jony Ive, are key to Corning's success in this evolving technological landscape.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs says to buy the dip in AI
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs's trading desk suggests buying the dip in AI-related stocks, despite recent declines, due to anticipated lower interest rates and strong fundamentals. The bank expects significant net income growth for AI companies and increased power generation tied to the technology, identifying a buying opportunity amidst what it perceives as excessive AI pessimism.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs top strategist: We’re on the precipice of a summer correction
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs's tactical strategist Scott Rubner warns of an impending summer correction for the S&P 500, citing historical data indicating a downturn typically begins around July 17th, followed by weak August outflows. He believes the "pain trade is no longer higher" as good news is already priced in, and systematic funds have reached maximum length. Rubner recommends put options on the Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 to clients.

feed.title: IBM eyes first record since 2013 as AI optimism fuels 19 percent rally
feed.summary: IBM has reportedly hit its highest stock price since 2013, nearing an all-time high, driven by investor optimism in its AI and cloud investments. Despite trailing Magnificent Seven stocks in five-year returns, IBM's 19% gain this year, fueled by strong Q4 2023 results and AI demand, positions it for potential re-rating. Wall Street remains divided on its growth prospects, but its robust 3.43% dividend yield sets it apart from many big tech companies.

feed.title: Apple supplier Jabil benches its CEO as it investigates his conduct—but he’ll still collect his full $1 million salary
feed.summary: Jabil, a key Apple supplier and technology manufacturer, has benched its CEO, Kenny Wilson, due to an investigation into his conduct, though he will continue to receive his full $1 million salary. The company's CFO, Michael Dastoor, has been appointed interim CEO as the probe, which is unrelated to financial statements, unfolds. This comes less than a year after Wilson took the helm, following a long career within the company.

feed.title: |让一部分人先看到未来
feed.summary: |The article covers several significant corporate and industry news items. Liangpin Puzi plans to sue an anti-counterfeiting internet celebrity, while Shanghai authorities investigate Pechoin cosmetics for alleged banned ingredients. Google addressed death threats from its AI chatbot, and the article also highlights major developments from SpaceX, Tencent's "Black Myth: Wukong" game, and financial reports from companies like Ctrip, Kingsoft, and XPeng.

feed.title: Government sues Ticketmaster owner and asks court to break up company's monopoly on live events
authors: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS
feed.summary: The Justice Department and 30 states have filed a sweeping antitrust lawsuit against Ticketmaster and its parent company, Live Nation Entertainment, accusing them of operating an illegal monopoly over live events. The lawsuit seeks to break up the company, alleging that its practices stifle competition, harm artists, and inflate ticket prices for consumers through excessive fees. Live Nation denies the allegations, stating that the lawsuit will not address the real issues affecting ticket prices and that competition has eroded Ticketmaster's market share.

feed.title: Microsoft on the Edge: Bounce Back or Breakdown? Gear Up for Earnings Showdown
feed.summary: This article from The Xchange Newsletter discusses upcoming Q3 earnings for various technology companies, including Microsoft. It highlights the potential for volatility around these earnings reports and suggests that traders should prepare for significant market movements. The piece also references several other articles related to earnings and market trends for major companies like Apple, Micron, Alphabet, and Nvidia.

feed.title: Diversification: Definition, How It Works
feed.summary: This article defines diversification as an investment strategy that spreads investment dollars across various assets to reduce risk and enhance potential returns. It explains how diversification minimizes the impact of market volatility and asset-specific risks by blending dissimilar assets like stocks and bonds across different industries, geographies, and company sizes. The piece also discusses methods for achieving diversification, including asset allocation, mutual funds, ETFs, target-date funds, and robo-advisors.

feed.title: Nvidia dethroned as world's most valuable company after just a few days on top
feed.summary: Nvidia's reign as the world's most valuable company was short-lived, with a two-day selloff erasing over $220 billion from its market capitalization and pushing it to third place behind Apple and Microsoft. Despite a 6.7% drop, analysts see no fundamental issues and expect any volatility to be temporary, reiterating "buy" ratings and raising price targets due to confidence in its AI leadership. The decline also coincided with broader market turbulence from a "triple-witching" options expiration.

feed.title: News Corp Initiates US$1 Billion Stock Buy-Back
feed.summary: News Corporation has announced a new repurchase program to buy back up to US$1 billion of its Class A and Class B common stock. This buy-back will be conducted sporadically in the open market or through other methods, excluding ASX-listed CDIs. The program is conditional on market factors and the stock's trading price, with 161,397,541 securities on issue in the class to be bought back.

feed.title: The 10 highest-paid CEOs
feed.summary: A new study by Equilar reveals that CEO compensation for S&P 500 companies surged by 12.6% in 2023, with median pay reaching $16.3 million, largely driven by stock awards. Broadcom's Hock Tan topped the list as the highest-paid CEO, earning over $161 million. The article details the top 10 highest-paid chief executives, including their companies, industries, and total compensation packages.

feed.title: Transact Campus Expands Across Canada with University of Victoria’s Mobile Credential Launch
feed.summary: Transact Campus has launched its Mobile Credential solution at the University of Victoria, making it the sixth major Canadian post-secondary institution to adopt this technology. This initiative aims to enhance campus experiences by providing seamless access to services via Apple and Google Wallet for students, faculty, and staff, aligning with the university's commitment to sustainability and modern student services. Transact is planning further expansion across Canada in 2025.

feed.title: Inflation is causing pet parents to pull back on treats, says J.M. Smucker’s CFO
feed.summary: J.M. Smucker's CFO, Tucker Marshall, notes that inflation is causing pet owners to reduce treat purchases and overall snacking, impacting the company's outlook despite strong growth in products like Uncrustables. The company achieved a 10% increase in quarterly profit per share, beating estimates, but faces challenges from rising coffee prices and reduced discretionary spending, leading to a revised lower sales forecast for the year.

feed.title: Nvidia, AMD Lead The Pack, Broadcom Stable, Intel Falters: Analyst's Latest Take
feed.summary: KeyBanc analyst John Vinh provided a mixed outlook for the semiconductor sector, with Nvidia and AMD showing strong performance and positive catalysts. Conversely, Qualcomm and Synaptics were downgraded due to increased competition and limited growth, while Intel, Micron, Marvell, and Monolithic Power Systems faced negative findings. Broadcom's stable outlook is tempered by potential future competition from Apple.

feed.title: Tandem Diabetes Care Launches Tandem Mobi, the World’s Smallest, Durable Automated Insulin Delivery System
feed.summary: Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (NASDAQ: TNDM) has announced the commercial launch of its new Tandem Mobi, the world's smallest durable automated insulin delivery system, now shipping to eligible customers in the United States. This system integrates the Control-IQ advanced hybrid closed-loop technology, offering improved diabetes management and flexibility through its compact design, various wearability options, and compatibility with the Dexcom G6 Continuous Glucose Monitoring System, with future integrations planned.

feed.title: DOJ's Visa antitrust lawsuit alleges debit card company monopoly
feed.summary: The Justice Department has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Visa, accusing the company of running a debit card monopoly that imposed billions of dollars in additional fees on American consumers and businesses. The lawsuit claims Visa stifles competition and charges excessive fees by controlling over 60% of U.S. debit transactions. Visa denies the allegations, stating its network offers security and value.

feed.title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway is buying up shares of a lagging 1990s dotcom darling
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested $74 million in VeriSign, despite the infrastructure company's stock falling 2% during a strong year for tech. Berkshire increased its stake by buying approximately 377,736 shares, making it VeriSign's largest shareholder with over 13 million shares worth about $2.6 billion. The investment is notable given VeriSign's high profit and operating margins and comes as Berkshire has been accumulating cash and selling off other stock holdings.

feed.title: 10 Cheat Codes I Learned While Scaling to $400M with Yext and Roam Founder Howard Lerman
feed.summary: Howard Lerman, founder of Yext and Roam, shares ten unconventional "cheat codes" that helped scale Yext to $400M. These strategies include memorizing your company's story, embracing risky live demos, rethinking traditional 1:1 meetings, and leveraging advisors over extensive outbound sales. Lerman emphasizes the importance of direct customer engagement, strategic board appointments, and prioritizing product-market fit above all else, while encouraging founders to have fun and construct their company their own way.

feed.title: Management That Matters: 2024’s Best U.S. Companies Unveiled
feed.summary: The 2024 Drucker Institute Company Rankings identify the 30 best-managed U.S. companies based on corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, financial strength, social responsibility, innovation, and employee engagement. Apple secured the top spot as the best-managed company with an effectiveness score of 92.1, surpassing Microsoft from its 2023 position. Nvidia and Microsoft followed, with Intel and MasterCard rounding out the top five, demonstrating the importance of innovation and financial strength for high effectiveness scores.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) Stock Topped the S&P 500 in 2024
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's Class A shares outperformed the S&P 500 in 2024, posting a 25.5% gain compared to the benchmark's 23.3%. This strong performance was driven by solid operating earnings, healthy investment income, and underwriting earnings from its insurance unit. Despite Warren Buffett's tempered expectations for future gains due to the company's size, Berkshire Hathaway has consistently doubled the S&P 500's average annual return since the 1960s.

feed.title: iShares Global Equity Factor ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the iShares Global Equity Factor ETF (GLOF), which was established in 2015 and focuses on total market equity. The ETF manages $203.3 million in assets across 651 holdings, selecting and weighting stocks based on quality, value, momentum, smaller size, and low volatility factors. The holdings list includes major companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft, along with numerous other global stocks and various currency cash positions.

feed.title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: December Score Edges Higher for Third Consecutive Month
feed.summary: The Schwab Trading Activity Index™ (STAX) increased to 51.16 in December, marking its third consecutive monthly rise. Schwab clients net bought equities, adding exposure in Health Care and Energy sectors, while selling Information Technology and Communication Services. The report highlights market volatility, key economic data releases, and popular stocks bought and sold by Schwab clients throughout December and the entirety of 2024.

feed.title: CES 2025: WD Unveils SanDisk Storage Solutions Designed for Creators and Gaming Enthusiasts
feed.summary: Western Digital (WD) unveiled new SanDisk storage solutions at CES 2025 catering to creators and gaming enthusiasts. The SanDisk Creator Series, featuring products like the Creator Phone SSD with MagSafe compatibility and various memory cards and drives, is designed for efficient content creation workflows. Additionally, WD introduced Fortnite-themed portable SSDs and USB flash drives, offering stylized storage for gamers.

feed.title: EWC, Canada ETFs Rise Amid Trudeau Resignation
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI Canada ETF (EWC) rose following the announcement of Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's resignation, citing a need for new leadership. The article also discusses why Canadian ETFs have historically lagged behind U.S. equity ETFs like SPY, attributing it to factors such as sector concentration, innovation, market size, currency effects, venture capital, and global influence.

feed.title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
feed.summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" stocks identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the US stock market's performance over the past five years. It suggests these stocks, including Nvidia, may continue their dominant trend into 2025 due to expected revenue growth. The piece also mentions other dominant stock groupings like "BATMMAAN" and the "Magnificent Seven."

feed.title: Most of the ‘Sweet 16’ stocks have dominated. They might do it again in 2025.
feed.summary: The article discusses the "Sweet 16" list of stocks, initially identified by Jefferies in September 2020, highlighting their significant contribution to the U.S. stock market's performance. The piece also mentions other groups of dominant stocks, such as "BATMMAAN," which includes Broadcom and the "Magnificent Seven."

feed.title: My smartwatch may have saved my life. Here's my story
feed.summary: A San Francisco Business Times senior reporter shares how his Fitbit smartwatch, which alerted him to irregular heart rhythms, potentially led to life-saving surgery. The article highlights the growing role of wearables like Fitbit and Apple Watch in detecting serious cardiac conditions such as Afib. It features related articles on medical advancements and AI in health, emphasizing the impact of technology on personal health management.

feed.title: Marathon Asset Management Provides $164M Mortgage Loan for Class A Mall in Thousand Oaks
feed.summary: Marathon Asset Management's Commercial Real Estate platform has provided a $164 million mortgage loan to Stockdale Capital Partners for their acquisition of The Oaks, a Class A regional mall in Thousand Oaks, CA. The 1.2 million square foot mall, anchored by Nordstrom, Dick’s Sporting Goods, and AMC Theaters, was acquired from Macerich. This financing enables Stockdale to execute their business plan by investing capital to update and improve the property, meeting local retail demand in the affluent Greater Los Angeles Area.

feed.title: Troubled EV maker Faraday Future is back (again) at CES
feed.summary: Faraday Future, the previously troubled EV maker, has resurfaced at CES with plans for a brand rebirth focusing on affordable electric minivans built with Chinese parts. While still offering its luxurious FF91, the company has launched a spin-off, FX, to produce models like the FX 5 ($20,000) and FX 6 ($30,000), aiming for production by late 2025. Additionally, Faraday Future plans to release a high-end electric minivan, the FX Super One, in Q2 2025, targeting celebrities and business professionals.

feed.title: Apple, Google And Meta Can't Escape Gambling Lawsuits, Judge Rejects Section 230 Shield In Landmark Ruling On Casino-Style Apps
feed.summary: A federal judge has ruled that Apple, Google, and Meta must face lawsuits alleging they profited from illegal casino-style apps, rejecting their Section 230 defense. The companies are accused of hosting and promoting addictive gambling apps and taking 30% commissions on in-app purchases, with plaintiffs seeking compensatory damages for alleged addiction and its consequences. This ruling adds to the growing legal scrutiny faced by tech giants.

feed.title: Fundsmith Equity Underperforms for the Fourth Year | Morningstar UK
feed.summary: Fundsmith Equity, the UK's largest fund, underperformed global markets for the fourth consecutive year in 2024, despite an 8.9% growth rate compared to the MSCI World Index's 20.8%. Fund manager Terry Smith attributed this to extreme market concentration in a few tech stocks and made several portfolio changes, including selling Diageo, McCormick, and Apple, and buying Atlas Copco and Texas Instruments. The article also discusses Smith's views on passive funds' role in AI growth and notes that despite recent underperformance, Fundsmith Equity still boasts strong long-term returns since its inception.

feed.title: Markets Brief: Why Good News Can Be Bad News for Stock Investors
feed.summary: US stock prices fell following stronger-than-expected employment numbers, highlighting investor concerns that positive economic news could lead to stickier inflation and fewer interest rate cuts in 2025. This downturn, particularly affecting high-growth technology companies whose valuations are sensitive to discount rates, suggests that value stocks may be better positioned. Upcoming inflation data and Taiwan Semiconductor earnings are expected to influence market volatility.

feed.title: VIDEO: ETF of the Week: FBCG
feed.summary: VettaFi's Head of Research, Todd Rosenbluth, discusses the Fidelity Blue Chip Growth ETF (FBCG) with Chuck Jaffe, highlighting its strong performance in 2023 and 2024. FBCG is presented as an actively managed, large-cap growth strategy with a proven track record, offering the efficiency and liquidity of an ETF. The discussion also touches upon "blue chip" as a definition of quality growth companies and the considerations for investors transitioning from the mutual fund version.

feed.title: Why a serial entrepreneur shops at Trader Joe’s, eschews a wristwatch, and believes the future of office work is in your computer
feed.summary: Howard Lerman, a serial entrepreneur and founder of Yext and Roam, shares insights into his "Good Life" and approach to work. He discusses his investment strategies, daily habits, and vision for the future of office work through his virtual office platform, Roam. Lerman emphasizes efficiency, a structured personal style, and continuous learning, while also revealing some of his luxury indulgences and travel experiences.

feed.title: Liberty All-Star Growth Fund Reports -3.94% December Drop, But 15.53% YTD Gain
feed.summary: Liberty All-Star Growth Fund (ASG) reported a -3.94% NAV and -3.75% market price performance for December 2024, despite ending the year with strong year-to-date gains of 15.53% (NAV) and 16.53% (market price). The fund's portfolio, with 97.7% invested in equities, was concentrated in Information Technology (29.5%), Health Care (16.8%), and Industrials (16.8%), holding top positions in NVIDIA, Apple, and Amazon. The fund executed significant portfolio adjustments in December and traded at a -7.4% discount to its NAV.

feed.title: Why Have Dividend Stock Strategies Been Underperforming?
feed.summary: Dividend stock strategies significantly underperformed the broader market in 2024, with the Morningstar Dividend Composite Index rising 15.7% compared to the Morningstar US Market Index's 24.1% gain. This underperformance is primarily attributed to the low exposure to technology stocks within dividend strategies, as tech and communication services were the best-performing sectors. Additionally, many top market contributors like Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon have low or no dividend payouts, favoring share repurchases as a way to return cash to shareholders, which dividend indexes typically do not capture.

feed.title: What’s new with Matter: how Matter 1.4 is reshaping interoperability and energy management
feed.summary: Matter 1.4 is the latest update to the smart home interoperability standard, introducing support for new device types like solar panels, heat pumps, and water heaters, alongside enhanced energy management features. This update also streamlines the multi-admin experience, allowing users to connect devices to several smart home systems more efficiently. While adoption is gradual, Matter 1.4 further solidifies the protocol's goal of creating a unified and user-friendly smart home ecosystem.

feed.title: NYSEARCA:QYLD - High-Yield ETF with 12% Annual Income: Is It Still Worth It?
feed.summary: The NYSEARCA:QYLD ETF provides a high annual yield exceeding 12% through a covered call strategy on NASDAQ 100 stocks, making it attractive for income-focused investors. However, this strategy caps upside potential, leading to significant underperformance compared to broader market indices and newer, more flexible alternatives like JEPQ. While it offers consistent monthly income, its long-term capital preservation issues and inability to fully capitalize on bull markets raise questions about its suitability for total return investors.

feed.title: Barclays, Synchrony Reportedly in Talks With Apple as Goldman Sachs Eyes Partnership
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs' CEO David Solomon indicated that their partnership with Apple for the Apple Card might conclude before 2030, despite a contract extending to that year. Reports suggest Apple is in discussions with Barclays and Synchrony Financial to potentially replace Goldman Sachs. The Apple Card has negatively impacted Goldman Sachs' Platform Solutions business, leading the firm to narrow its strategic focus while seeing revenue growth in other segments.

feed.title: Study says Medtronic Linq cardiac monitors successfully predict risk thresholds for patients with AFib
feed.summary: A new study found that Medtronic's Linq family of insertable cardiac monitors (ICMs) successfully predict high-risk patients for AFib-related healthcare utilization. The DEFINE AFib study showed Linq ICMs had four times greater episode sensitivity than the Apple Watch and could predict a patient's risk of needing AFib-related healthcare 80% of the time, highlighting the superior accuracy of medical-grade technology for chronic condition management.

feed.title: Barclays and Synchrony Financial in the running to take over Apple's credit card business
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly in talks with Barclays and Synchrony Financial to take over its credit card business, currently handled by Goldman Sachs. Goldman Sachs has been seeking to exit the consumer market, facing significant losses and recent regulatory penalties. This move follows Barclays' previous acquisition of General Motors' card business from Goldman Sachs.

feed.title: Eligible Synchrony Mastercard Customers Now Have Access to the Pay Later Feature of Apple Pay
feed.summary: Synchrony (NYSE: SYF) is now allowing eligible Synchrony Mastercard holders to access a "Pay Later" feature for purchases made through Apple Pay on iPhone and iPad. This enhancement provides customers with the option to choose fixed monthly payments or standard credit card terms at checkout. Synchrony plans to expand this promotional offer to more co-branded cards and will enable rewards redemption for eligible cards via Apple Pay later in the year, aiming to offer greater flexibility in digital payments.

feed.title: Exclusive: Apple in talks with Barclays, Synchrony to replace Goldman in credit card deal, sources say
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly in discussions with Barclays and Synchrony Financial to potentially replace Goldman Sachs as its credit card partner, according to sources familiar with the matter. This comes as Goldman Sachs plans to scale back its consumer finance operations, despite its existing credit card deal with Apple lasting until 2030. Other financial firms are interested, although some previously viewed the initial deal terms as unprofitable.

feed.title: Apple has worst day since August following reports of China, AI struggles
feed.summary: Apple stock experienced its worst day since August, dropping 4% on Thursday due to poor iPhone sales reports in China and analyst concerns about AI not boosting demand. Its stock is down nearly 12% from its December peak, making it the worst-performing major tech stock in 2025 so far. Analysts predict a decline in iPhone shipments for the first half of 2025, with Apple Intelligence not yet proving beneficial for hardware replacement cycles.

feed.title: IMAX Secures Netflix Deal for Exclusive 'Narnia' Theatrical Release, Stock Jumps
feed.summary: IMAX has announced a deal with Netflix to exclusively release Greta Gerwig's "Narnia" film across its global theater network for two weeks starting Thanksgiving 2026, before it premieres on Netflix at Christmas 2026. This move, which caused IMAX stock to jump, marks a rare theatrical-first strategy for Netflix, known for premiering films directly on its platform. The collaboration follows Netflix's previous efforts to attract subscribers through premium sports content and its crackdown on password sharing.

feed.title: MicroStrategy Sets Sights on Amazon and Alphabet’s Common Shares Dominance
feed.summary: MicroStrategy is seeking shareholder approval to dramatically increase its authorized Class A common shares from 330 million to 10.3 billion, a move that would position it with more shares outstanding than many Nasdaq 100 tech giants. This expansion is designed to fuel its aggressive Bitcoin acquisition strategy, which has already seen its stock surge over 2,500% since 2020. Despite potential dilution concerns, investors appear to prioritize the company's Bitcoin-centric growth.

feed.title: Apple Card Could Find New Home With Barclays or Synchrony. What Cardholders Should Know
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly in discussions with Barclays and Synchrony Financial to potentially replace Goldman Sachs as the issuer for the Apple Card. This comes amid Goldman Sachs' withdrawal from consumer lending and financial losses, despite the Apple Card's high customer satisfaction ratings. A new issuer could bring significant changes for cardholders, potentially adding competitive features like welcome offers and better perks, which the current card largely lacks.

feed.title: Spotify: Strong Growth, Improving Profits Make It A Great Long-Term Buy (NYSE:SPOT)
feed.summary: Spotify is showing strong profitable growth, marked by record-high gross margins, solid cash flow, and strategic expansions into various audio content. Despite a high Price-to-Sales ratio, its rapid revenue growth, improved profitability, and free cash flow justify its premium valuation. Key drivers include increased ad revenue, international market expansion, and advanced ad-targeting tools, though risks like licensing costs, competition, and regulatory scrutiny remain.

feed.title: Sonos takeover speculation grows, with Spotify or Amazon tipped as potential suitors (report)
feed.summary: Speculation is mounting that audio technology company Sonos could be acquired following a problematic app rollout in 2024 and recent leadership changes, including the departure of CEO Patrick Spence. Bloomberg's Mark Gurman identifies Spotify and Amazon as the most likely suitors. While Amazon could integrate Sonos' premium audio into its Echo devices, Spotify could gain immediate entry into the hardware market, despite its past struggles with products like the "Car Thing."

feed.title: Should Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) Be on Your Investing Radar?
feed.summary: The Invesco Dividend Achievers ETF (PFM) is a passively managed large-cap value ETF with $719.98 million in assets, sponsored by Invesco. Launched in 2005, it focuses on dividend-paying companies that have increased dividends for over 10 consecutive years, with top holdings including Broadcom, Apple, and Microsoft. PFM has a Zacks ETF Rank of 3 (Hold) and an expense ratio of 0.52%, and its performance has shown year-to-date gains of 2.90% and a 19.34% increase over the last year as of January 22, 2025.

feed.title: IUSV: A Cost-Effective Value ETF With Strong Downside Protection And Growth Potential
feed.summary: The iShares Core S&P U.S. Value ETF (IUSV) offers cost-efficient value investing with a portfolio of 700 U.S. large-cap and mid-cap value stocks and a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its significant allocation to large-cap stocks and a 23% exposure to the technology sector, including Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, contribute to strong downside protection and growth potential. IUSV has demonstrated superior performance, outperforming its peer IWD with a 167.2% total return over the past decade and 597.5% since inception.

feed.title: Major San Francisco Union Square hotel to hit the market as part of $1B sale
feed.summary: The Grand Hyatt San Francisco in Union Square is set to be put on the market by Host Hotels & Resorts Inc. as part of a larger $1 billion sale of West Coast properties. This divestment is consistent with Host's plan to offload "noncore" assets, including the 669-room hotel, which was acquired in a $1 billion three-hotel deal in 2018. The sale follows a recent strike by hotel workers and another significant change in San Francisco hotel ownership.

feed.title: SanDisk: new storage solutions for smartphones
feed.summary: Western Digital announced the SanDisk Creator Series at CES 2025, offering new storage solutions specifically designed for content creators, particularly those using smartphones. The series includes products like the SanDisk Creator Phone SSD with MagSafe compatibility, Creator microSD cards, SD UHS-II cards, Phone Drives, USB-C Flash Drives, Pro Portable SSDs, and a Desk Drive, all aimed at providing speed, capacity, durability, and portability for demanding creative workflows. Additionally, the SanDisk Extreme PRO Dual Drive was unveiled, offering high-speed transfers with both USB-A and USB-C connections.

feed.title: Tech’s TikTok Dilemma: Follow the Law or President Trump? (Published 2025)
feed.summary: A federal law banning TikTok in the U.S. has created a schism among American tech companies, forcing them to choose between complying with the law or adhering to President-elect Trump's executive order to pause its enforcement. Companies like Amazon, Apple, and Google have moved to comply with the ban, while others like Oracle, Akamai, and Fastly continue to support TikTok. This situation highlights the legal and political complexities surrounding the TikTok ban and the varying approaches taken by major tech firms.

feed.title: Webull Thailand Named Best Online Trading Platform for US Market Access by The Global Economics
feed.summary: Webull Thailand has been recognized as the "Best Online Trading Platform for US Market Access" by The Global Economics, highlighting its rapid growth and commitment to innovative investment solutions. Since its launch, Webull Thailand has attracted over 300,000 registered users and was the most downloaded brokerage app in Thailand during November and December 2024. The platform offers unique features such as 24-hour trading of US stocks and ETFs and US options trading, aiming to connect Thai investors to global opportunities.

feed.title: Retire rich with these blue-chip dividend ETFs
feed.summary: This article identifies top blue-chip dividend ETFs, including JEPQ, VYM, DGRO, DTD, and SDY, as tools for investors seeking a rich retirement through regular income and strong returns. It highlights their diversification benefits over individual stocks but notes that many dividend ETFs often underperform benchmark indices like the S&P 500 in terms of total returns. The article provides details on the yield, holdings, and performance of each recommended ETF.

feed.title: Exclusive: White House in talks to have Oracle and U.S. investors take over TikTok
feed.summary: The White House is negotiating a deal where Oracle and U.S. investors would take majority control of TikTok's global operations, with Oracle overseeing the app's algorithm, data collection, and software updates. This plan aims to address concerns about the app's Chinese ownership by minimizing ByteDance's stake and ensuring American oversight. This negotiation follows a congressional mandate for TikTok to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a nationwide ban.

feed.title: Printed Circuit Board (PCB) Market to Grow by USD 26.8 Billion (2025-2029), Driven by Smartphone Adoption, Report Highlights AI's Redefining Role - Technavio
feed.summary: The global Printed Circuit Board (PCB) market is projected to grow by USD 26.8 billion between 2025 and 2029, driven primarily by the rising adoption of smartphones and increasing industry automation, with a CAGR of over 6.2%. Technavio's report, which utilizes AI-powered analysis, highlights the significant role of PCBs in various electronic devices, 5G technology, IoT, and automotive applications. Despite environmental concerns regarding PCB production and disposal, the demand for multi-layer, HDI, and flexible PCBs is expected to escalate due to the need for compact and high-performance electronic devices.

feed.title: Data Center Market to Grow by USD 535.6 Billion (2025-2029), Driven by Multi-Cloud Adoption and Network Upgrades, Report on AI's Impact on Market Trends - Technavio
feed.summary: The global data center market is projected to grow by USD 535.6 billion between 2025 and 2029, achieving a CAGR of 15.6%. This growth is primarily fueled by the increasing adoption of multi-cloud strategies and necessary network upgrades, alongside a significant trend of integrating AI into data center operations. However, cybersecurity concerns remain a key challenge for the market.

feed.title: XLK vs. QQQ: Which Tech ETF Should You Buy for Your Portfolio?
feed.summary: This article analyzes two popular tech-heavy ETFs, XLK and QQQ, comparing their long-term performance, expense ratios, dividends, and portfolio allocations. While both have outperformed the S&P 500, XLK focuses purely on tech, making it more concentrated in top tech stocks like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. QQQ offers broader diversification by including communication services and consumer cyclicals, which may appeal to investors seeking less concentration in the top three tech giants.

feed.title: AI stock rout: The US selloff in 4 charts
feed.summary: US stocks experienced a selloff, particularly in large-cap growth, following news that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek developed an advanced AI model that requires less sophisticated semiconductor chips. This development triggered concerns about the necessity of significant capital expenditures by large US tech companies in generative AI, leading to a 17% plunge in Nvidia stock and declines in other chipmakers and utility stocks. However, some tech giants like Apple and Meta Platforms saw gains, and Morningstar analysts maintain their fair value estimates for Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet, expecting long-term benefits from reduced AI model pricing.

feed.title: Meta Posts Q4 Earnings Beat, But Reality Labs Losses Mount
feed.summary: Meta Platforms reported strong Q4 2024 earnings, exceeding analysts' expectations in revenue and EPS, driven by its Family of Apps (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp). However, the Reality Labs division continued to incur substantial operating losses, raising concerns about the metaverse's future and prompting significant AI infrastructure investments. Despite these losses, Meta's shares performed well, indicating investor confidence in Zuckerberg's overall strategy.

feed.title: Qorvo: Solid Earnings Beat, But ACG Division Pressures Are Likely To Persist (NASDAQ:QRVO)
feed.summary: Qorvo (QRVO) has underperformed over the past year, losing 17% despite a solid earnings beat in Q3-25. While the company disposed of its SiC power business and lifted its Q4 EPS outlook, its ACG division, which accounts for 70% of group sales, is expected to face ongoing challenges. The article concludes that valuations are not cheap and technical chart conditions are unfavorable, suggesting persistent pressure on the stock.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL) Gets Another Downgrade on iPhone Risk Ahead of Results
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) has received another downgrade, this time from Oppenheimer, citing iPhone risk and a lack of compelling AI applications. This marks at least the fifth downgrade for Apple this month, placing it among the least-loved megacap stocks apart from Tesla Inc. The downgrades come ahead of the company's upcoming earnings results.

feed.title: DeepSeek Panic Live Updates: Nvidia Stock Drops 4% As Trump Reportedly Targets China Exports
