These actively managed funds aim to generate 1.2 times the weekly returns of their underlying companies, providing income-focused investors with amplified returns and potential tax advantages through return of capital distributions. This innovation expands Roundhill's portfolio of weekly income strategies, with plans for additional leveraged WeeklyPay ETFs covering other major companies.

feed.title: Mark Zuckerberg Offloads Meta Shares Worth $14.4 Million In February
feed.summary: Mark Zuckerberg sold 17,855 shares of Meta Platforms Inc. Class A common stock worth approximately $14.4 million on February 14, at prices between $726.68 and $740.71. These transactions were executed under a pre-established trading plan and follow previous sales in late December 2024. Despite these sales, Meta demonstrates strong financial health with a high gross profit margin and increased quarterly dividends.

feed.title: AAPW Stock Fund Price and Chart — CBOE:AAPW
feed.summary: The article provides a detailed overview of the Roundhill AAPL WeeklyPay ETF (AAPW), highlighting its price performance, key statistics like assets under management and dividend yield, and investment strategy. AAPW aims to offer weekly income and leveraged exposure (120%) to AAPL stock, emphasizing its short-term tactical nature and associated risks due to lack of diversification and leverage. It also includes frequently asked questions about ETFs, AAPW's specific metrics, and technical analysis.

feed.title: Clean200 list shows sustainable companies on path to dominate global economy
feed.summary: The 2025 Clean200 list, compiled by As You Sow and Corporate Knights, highlights 200 publicly traded companies leading the sustainable clean energy economy. Despite political attacks, these companies, including Apple, Microsoft, and Tesla, generated $2.5 trillion in sustainable revenue and outperformed fossil fuel stocks and the global benchmark MSCI ACWI since 2016. The report emphasizes that the clean energy economy is demonstrating robust growth and is on track to dominate the global economy.

feed.title: Learning curves: The future of pilot training with AI, flight simulators and digital training
feed.summary: The article discusses the evolution of pilot training due to the global pilot shortage, emphasizing the integration of AI, flight simulators, and digital training methods. It highlights how training providers like BAA Training, Skyborne Academy, and CAE are adopting hybrid approaches, VR, AR, and data analytics to enhance learning efficiency, maintain safety standards, and address the growing demand for new pilots while ensuring competency in both automated and manual flight skills. Regulators like the UK CAA and FAA are closely monitoring these advancements to ensure high standards are maintained.

feed.title: Chip design software maker Cadence revenue skyrockets with AI demand
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems reported strong Q4 2024 revenues, up 27% to $1.36 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-driven chip design software and expanded partnerships with major chipmakers like Nvidia and Intel. Despite robust growth and a 2024 fiscal year revenue of $4.6 billion, the company's 2025 financial outlook fell below Wall Street expectations due to uncertainties in its China business amid evolving trade restrictions. Cadence expects flat growth in China for 2025 and plans to be prudent with its guidance given the market's unpredictability.

feed.title: AAPW: Roundhill AAPL WeeklyPay ETF Latest Stock Price, Analysis, News and Trading Ideas
feed.summary: This page provides an overview of the Roundhill AAPL WeeklyPay ETF (AAPW), including its latest stock price, key financial metrics like 52-week high/low, and daily trading volume. It also offers sections for news, sentiment analysis, fundamentals, and community discussions related to AAPW, though much of this content appears to be dynamically loaded or unavailable. A sentiment poll regarding memory stocks is also featured.

feed.title: YOKE Core ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the YOKE Core ETF, established on February 21, 2025. YOKE is an actively managed fund with $229.66 million in assets under management and 52 holdings, focusing on total market equity through dynamic tactical allocation. The fund employs strategies centered on momentum, quality, and risk sentiment, and the article lists its primary holdings including major companies like Alphabet, NVIDIA, and Apple.

feed.title: Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (FYEE)
feed.summary: MarketBeat has provided a review and aggregate rating for the Fidelity Yield Enhanced Equity ETF (FYEE). Based on analyst ratings for its 24 top holdings, FYEE has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $28.80. The article details the consensus ratings and price targets for the ETF's main components, highlighting key companies like NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft.

feed.title: Berkshire’s Earnings: Diamond Hands or Paper Hands?
feed.summary: This article examines the upcoming Berkshire Hathaway earnings report in late February, outlining both bullish and bearish catalysts for its stock (BRK.B). It highlights Berkshire's strategic investments, record cash reserves, and strong operating businesses as bullish factors, while portfolio reductions in Apple and Bank of America, legal challenges, and leadership transition uncertainty are identified as bearish concerns. The article emphasizes Berkshire's role as a diversified, non-tech investment with exposure to financials, energy, and industrials, suggesting that the earnings call will provide critical insights into its future direction.

feed.title: Apple to invest more than $500B in US manufacturing
feed.summary: Apple Inc. announced a significant investment of over $500 billion into U.S. manufacturing over the next four years, aiming to boost domestic job creation and manufacturing capabilities. This commitment includes opening a new server facility in Houston by 2026 for its AI systems, doubling its U.S. Advanced Manufacturing Fund to $10 billion, and establishing a training academy in Detroit. The move, seen by some as a response to potential tariffs and a win for the current administration, will also expand Apple's data center capacity and support silicon engineering.

feed.title: Quality Power drops 10% on mkt debut; Mirae Asset MF launches two ETFs
feed.summary: Shares of Quality Power Electrical Equipments fell nearly 10 percent on its market debut, closing at ₹384, down from its issue price of ₹425. The company's ₹634 crore IPO had been subscribed 1.3 times. Separately, Mirae Asset Mutual Fund launched two new Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs), the BSE Select IPO ETF and the BSE 200 Equal Weight ETF, along with corresponding fund of funds to invest in them.

feed.title: Palantir, Super Micro, Nvidia, Alibaba, Berkshire Hathaway, Rivian, Apple, and More Stock Movers
feed.summary: A slide in technology stocks pulled down the S&P 500 on Monday, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 1.2%. This decline followed a report that Microsoft had cancelled data-center leases, leading to a broader sell-off in the tech sector. Key movers included Palantir, Super Micro, Nvidia, Alibaba, Berkshire Hathaway, Rivian, and Apple.

feed.title: Socket Mobile Announces Industrial Scanning Solutions for iPhone 16e
feed.summary: Socket Mobile announced that its industrial scanning solutions, XtremeScan and DuraSled, are compatible with the new iPhone 16e, a durable and cost-effective device ideal for industrial environments. These solutions provide integrated 1D/2D barcode scanning capabilities, transforming the iPhone 16e into a powerful data capture tool for sectors like warehousing, logistics, and manufacturing. The compatibility ensures businesses can leverage the iPhone's iOS interface with Socket Mobile's robust scanning technology, supported by their CaptureSDK.

feed.title: Apple’s $500B US expansion includes chips from TSMC Phoenix
feed.summary: Apple pledged $500 billion for US expansion, including sourcing chips from TSMC's Phoenix factory, where mass production began recently. This investment is set to create 20,000 new US jobs and expand manufacturing and data centers across several states. Former President Trump and the Biden administration both claim credit for stimulating domestic chip production, with Trump citing tariffs and Biden highlighting the CHIPS and Science Act.

feed.title: Bohemian women's clothing retailer Free People to open a store in downtown Detroit
feed.summary: Free People, a bohemian women's clothing retailer owned by Urban Outfitters Inc., is opening a new store at 1425 Woodward Ave. in downtown Detroit, in the former Busted Bra Shop location. This new store joins other recently announced retailers in the area, including Gardner White and an Apple Store, signaling continued retail development in downtown Detroit. Free People also has two other Michigan locations, in Troy and Grand Rapids.

feed.title: What's Going On With FiscalNote Holdings Stock Monday?
feed.summary: FiscalNote Holdings Inc (NYSE: NOTE) saw its shares trading higher after announcing a definitive agreement to divest two non-core assets, Oxford Analytica and Dragonfly, to Dow Jones for $40 million. This move is expected to reduce the company's senior term loan, strengthen its balance sheet, and streamline operations, focusing on core Policy solutions. Additionally, FiscalNote engaged John Lee, former head of contracts for Palantir’s U.S. government vertical, as a senior strategic advisor to support its AI and data-driven solutions for the public sector.

feed.title: Globalstar: D2D Concerns Are A Distraction (NASDAQ:GSAT)
feed.summary: Investor confidence in Globalstar has been undermined by the announcement that iPhones will access Starlink's D2D service. However, the author argues this is a distraction, given Globalstar's partnership with Apple and the impending inflection point for XCOM RAN technology. Significant improvements in Globalstar's balance sheet and cash flows are anticipated, especially with growth in IoT and private networks, making its high revenue multiple justifiable due to high EBITDA margins and growth potential.

feed.title: Apple to build manufacturing facility in Texas
feed.summary: Apple is investing $500 billion in the U.S. over the next four years, including a planned 250,000-square-foot computer server production facility in Houston, Texas. This initiative aims to support American innovation, create jobs, and enhance the company's sustainability efforts by utilizing recycled and renewable materials in its products. The investment also covers AI, silicon engineering, and skills development, alongside expanding data center capacities and establishing a manufacturing academy.

feed.title: Apple Plans Houston Plant to Produce AI Servers
feed.summary: Apple Inc. plans to establish a new 250,000-sq-ft manufacturing facility in Houston, Texas, to produce servers for its Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute AI services, with an expected opening next year. This initiative is part of a larger $500 billion investment in the U.S. over four years, including expanding data centers and doubling its "Advanced Manufacturing Fund" to $10 billion. The move comes as companies face new tariffs on goods imported from China and indicates a shift towards more U.S.-based manufacturing, potentially with Foxconn identified as the manufacturer for the Houston plant.

feed.title: Apple commits to 'largest ever' $500bn US investment
feed.summary: Apple announced a multi-year plan to invest over $500 billion in the US and create 20,000 new jobs, with a focus on research, development, software, and AI. This commitment includes building a new manufacturing factory in Texas to support its AI system, Apple Intelligence, and expanding data centers across several states. The announcement comes after Apple CEO Tim Cook met with President Donald Trump, who has consistently pushed for increased domestic manufacturing.

feed.title: Max Streaming Service to Bow in Australia on March 31
feed.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery will launch its Max streaming service in Australia on March 31, 2026. The service will offer a wide array of premium content from brands like HBO, Warner Bros., and DC Universe, with various subscription tiers including an ad-supported option. A partnership with Foxtel will also provide subscribers with access to the Max app at no additional cost.

feed.title: Apple’s $500B pledge includes chips from massive TSMC Phoenix factory; Trump credits tariff threat
feed.summary: Apple has pledged $500 billion for investment in the U.S., with a portion directed towards the Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) factory in Phoenix, where mass production of Apple chips has already begun. Former President Donald Trump has claimed credit for this development, attributing it to his past tariff threats, while the Biden administration supported domestic chip production through the CHIPS and Science Act. The TSMC factory, representing a $65 billion foreign investment, is set to create thousands of jobs and produce advanced chips for various technologies.

feed.title: NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH) Price, Holdings, & News
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information about the NEOS Nasdaq-100 Hedged Equity Income ETF (QQQH), including its current price, key statistics, overview, and expense ratios. It also lists recent news headlines related to QQQH and answers frequently asked questions about its performance and share activity. The ETF aims to generate current income with downside protection by investing in Nasdaq-100 stocks combined with an options collar.

feed.title: 98% of John Deere & Apple Shareholders Reject Anti-Diversity Proposals
feed.summary: Shareholders of Deere & Co (NYSE:DE) and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) overwhelmingly rejected shareholder proposals aimed at undermining diversity and inclusion programs, with votes against reaching over 98% at both companies. This follows a similar rejection at Costco, indicating a strong shareholder belief that diversity and inclusion are material to profitable growth and financial outperformance. Andrew Behar, CEO of As You Sow, stated that these votes signal a rejection of attempts to inject political culture wars into standard business practices, reinforcing the business rationale for diverse workforces.

feed.title: How Apple’s iconic products reshaped everyday life: A look back at decades of innovation
feed.summary: This article chronicles how Apple's key product releases, from the Apple II to AirPods, have profoundly changed daily life over several decades. It highlights how Apple consistently redefined technology markets, even when not being the first to introduce a product. The piece emphasizes the company's strong brand loyalty and its focus on both design and functionality to revolutionize user experience.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Made $2.5 Billion Today. Here’s How He Did It
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's net worth increased by $2.6 billion due to a 1.6% rise in Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) stock during midday trading on Thursday, February 27. Despite this significant paper gain, the article emphasizes that for Buffett, this is a minor fluctuation and he focuses on long-term value, as evidenced by Berkshire's $334.2 billion cash hoard signaling caution in the market. The increase is attributed to a post-earnings bounce and broader market cheer, with most of Berkshire's portfolio stocks performing positively.

feed.title: Apple and John Deere reject anti-DEI proposals amid corporate walkbacks
feed.summary: Apple and John Deere shareholders have voted against anti-DEI proposals, contrasting with other companies that are dismantling or softening their diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts amidst a changing political and legal landscape. A recent executive order and Department of Justice memo signal federal opposition to DEI programs, prompting many brands like Bank of America, Google, and Disney to walk back their initiatives. However, some companies like e.l.f. and Patagonia are reaffirming their DEI commitments, while those that have scaled back, like Target, have faced public criticism and boycotts.

feed.title: FDVV vs. SCHD: Which Dividend ETF is the Smartest Buy in 2025?
feed.summary: This article compares two dividend ETFs, FDVV and SCHD, to determine which is a smarter buy in 2025 based on growth, yield, and value. SCHD offers a higher dividend yield and stronger historical dividend growth, with a defensive sector allocation, making it suitable for income stability. FDVV, while having a lower yield and slower dividend growth, benefits from significant exposure to high-growth technology stocks, appealing to investors seeking dividend exposure with tech upside and willing to accept higher volatility.

feed.title: Gravity Sets to Strengthen Ragnarok IP's Dominance in Southeast Asia in 2025
feed.summary: Global gaming company Gravity is expanding its presence in Southeast Asia by launching four new Ragnarok IP titles in the first half of 2025: Ragnarok M: Classic, Ragnarok Idle Adventure Plus, Ragnarok V: Returns, and Ragnarok: Back to Glory. These new titles aim to build on the franchise's significant success and popularity in the region since its initial entry in 2003, offering diverse gameplay experiences for players. Gravity's Business Director, Kim Jin-hwan, expressed confidence that these distinct titles will capture player interest and further solidify Ragnarok's dominance.

feed.title: Apple's new Houston facility could start a renaissance for electronics companies
feed.summary: Apple's decision to build a new AI server manufacturing facility in Houston could signal a resurgence for the region's electronics manufacturing sector, which previously thrived with companies like Texas Instruments and Compaq. The facility, part of a $500 billion investment in U.S. technology, will assemble servers crucial for Apple Intelligence, and is expected to bring thousands of jobs. This move, facilitated by Foxconn, may also help Apple navigate potential tariffs on foreign imports and aims to bring overseas manufacturing back to the U.S.

feed.title: The Fintiv Pendulum Swings Again: More Discretionary Denials Coming Soon
feed.summary: Acting USPTO Director Coke Stewart has rescinded the 2022 Vidal memorandum, which had limited discretionary denials of PTAB post-grant proceedings. This policy shift is expected to lead to a significant increase in discretionary denials of IPR institution petitions, returning greater discretion to the PTAB in cases with parallel district court litigation. The move emphasizes reliance on established PTAB precedent, particularly decisions in Apple Inc. v. Fintiv, Inc. and Sotera Wireless, Inc. v. Masimo Corp., and suggests a notable change in the landscape for patent challengers.

feed.title: Milberg Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Tracking Users Without Consent
feed.summary: Milberg has filed a class action lawsuit against Meta, alleging that the social media giant bypasses user privacy settings by directing them to Meta's in-app browser to track their online activity. The lawsuit claims Meta uses JavaScript injection to monitor user interactions on third-party websites without consent, despite users' privacy preferences. This legal action seeks compensatory damages and injunctive relief for a national class, a California subclass, and an iOS subclass of Meta users.

feed.title: QQQX: 8%-Plus Yield, 9% Discount, Long-Term NAV Growth (NASDAQ:QQQX)
feed.summary: QQQX is a closed-end fund that generates an 8%+ yield by selling covered calls on NASDAQ 100 stocks, aiming for attractive returns with reduced volatility. The fund boasts a 35% NAV/share increase since inception and currently trades at a 9.11% discount to NAV, providing regular quarterly distributions with potential tax advantages. Despite risks like price volatility and call option risks, QQQX is considered a speculative Buy for investors seeking high yield.

feed.title: Will Apple's $500 Billion U.S. Manufacturing Investment Make a Difference for American Workers?
feed.summary: Apple announced a $500 billion investment over four years to expand U.S. facilities, aiming to create 20,000 American jobs, including a new advanced manufacturing facility in Houston for AI servers. However, critics, like the Alliance for American Manufacturing President Scott Paul, suggest this investment, while substantial, is relatively minor for a company of Apple's size and doesn't address the core issue of offshoring iPhone production. The article contends that Apple's latest announcement is consistent with past patterns of making similar U.S. investment claims without significantly altering its manufacturing footprint for its primary products.

feed.title: Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announces new $100B investment in US
feed.summary: Taiwanese chip giant TSMC announced a new $100 billion investment in the U.S. for three additional advanced semiconductor manufacturing plants, bringing their total commitment to $165 billion. CEO C.C. Wei credited President Donald Trump's vision for these investments, which will include an R&D center in Arizona. This expansion follows TSMC's existing $65 billion commitment under the CHIPS and Science Act and comes amidst discussions about avoiding potential U.S. tariffs on foreign-made chips.

feed.title: Apple defeats US consumer lawsuit over iCloud storage, for now
feed.summary: Apple has successfully dismissed a consumer lawsuit alleging monopolization of the digital storage market through its iCloud service, though the plaintiffs have been granted leave to amend and refile their complaint. U.S. District Judge Eumi Lee ruled that the plaintiffs failed to show Apple violated antitrust law or held monopoly power, and that consumers are not obligated to purchase iCloud storage. The lawsuit, filed in March 2024, sought to represent millions of consumers paying for iCloud storage, arguing Apple coerced users into using its service for core data backups.

feed.title: Apple introduces iPad Air with powerful M3 chip and new Magic Keyboard
feed.summary: Apple has unveiled a new iPad Air featuring the powerful M3 chip, delivering significant performance enhancements and Apple Intelligence integration. Available in 11-inch and 13-inch models, the device also introduces a redesigned Magic Keyboard and supports Apple Pencil Pro, targeting content creators, students, and travelers. Alongside the new iPad Air, Apple updated its standard iPad with the A16 chip and doubled its starting storage, while also highlighting the environmental considerations in both products.

feed.title: First Class Action Filed Under Washington’s MY Health MY Data Act Draws Parallels to Previous SDK Litigation
feed.summary: The first class action lawsuit under Washington's MY Health MY Data Act (MHMDA) has been filed against Amazon, alleging improper collection and use of consumer location data via Software Development Kits (SDKs). The lawsuit, Maxwell v. Amazon.com, Inc., draws parallels to prior SDK class actions, but introduces MHMDA's broad private right of action for violations related to "consumer health data," which includes precise location information. Businesses are advised to be mindful of SDK use as more states enact privacy legislation and similar lawsuits are expected.

feed.title: Federal Circuit Affirms Partial Win for Patent Owner Against Apple
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals (CAFC) has upheld a mixed ruling from the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) concerning Gesture Technology's patent on camera sensing technology. While some claims of the patent were deemed unpatentable, others were not, leading to an appeal by Apple and a cross-appeal by Gesture. The CAFC rejected Apple's arguments regarding the legal standard for obviousness and Gestures's estoppel claim, ultimately affirming the PTAB's decision.

feed.title: Alphabet's Google urges US government to avoid breaking up firm, source says
feed.summary: Alphabet's Google reportedly met with the Trump administration, urging them to halt efforts to break up the company. The U.S. Department of Justice is pursuing two antitrust cases against Google, one over search and another over advertising technology, with a trial on remedies expected in April. Industry experts suggest President Trump may ease some antitrust policies from the previous administration, potentially impacting the push to dismantle Google.

feed.title: Apple unveils new Mac Studio, the most powerful Mac ever, featuring M4 Max and new M3 Ultra
feed.summary: Apple has announced the new Mac Studio, powered by the M4 Max and the new M3 Ultra chips, making it the most powerful Mac ever created. This professional desktop offers groundbreaking performance with extensive connectivity, including Thunderbolt 5, and supports up to 512GB of unified memory and 16TB of SSD storage. It is designed to handle intensive AI workloads and is built for Apple Intelligence, with availability starting March 12th.

feed.title: The company that commissioned Wall Street's 'Fearless Girl' statue radically adjusts diversity policies for its index funds
feed.summary: State Street, known for commissioning the "Fearless Girl" statue, has reversed its diversity policies for companies in its index funds. Previously requiring 30% female board representation, the company now encourages boards to determine their own composition with diverse experiences and perspectives. This move, which also drops requirements for DEI goals and disclosure of gender/racial board makeup, aligns with a broader trend of companies walking back DEI programs.

feed.title: Apple Debuts New MacBook Air, Mac Studio With AI-Ready Chips
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has launched updated MacBook Air laptops and Mac Studio desktops featuring new AI-focused M4 processors. The MacBook Air models come in 13-inch and 15-inch sizes, start at lower prices, and include improved cameras and multi-monitor support. These updates aim to sustain the recent sales resurgence for the company's computer line.

feed.title: Apple’s Use of SiTime in iPhone 16e Fuels Stock Rally for Chipmaker
feed.summary: SiTime Corp. experienced a 20% stock rally on Wednesday after its timer chips were discovered in the new iPhone 16e during a device teardown by TechInsights. This news has boosted the profile of the relatively unknown chipmaker, which reported approximately $200 million in revenue last year. The integration of SiTime's components in the iPhone 16e signifies a broader shift in the device's internal architecture, benefiting the chipmaker.

feed.title: Del Monte Shopping Center trades hands for $123.535M
feed.summary: JLL Capital Markets has announced the sale of Del Monte Shopping Center in Monterey, California, for $123.535 million. American Assets Trust, Inc. sold the 675,088-square-foot retail property to Federal Realty Investment Trust. The center, a cornerstone of Monterey's retail landscape since 1967, is 83.2% occupied and attracts 5.2 million annual visitors with a diverse mix of high-profile tenants.

feed.title: Apple And The UK Government Broke A “Trust Promise”
feed.summary: Apple has ceased offering its Advanced Data Protection (ADP) feature in the UK, a decision prompted by the UK government's request for access to user data. This move is seen as a breach of trust by Apple, which traditionally champions user privacy, and raises concerns about global repercussions for data privacy. The article emphasizes that this decision could further erode already weak public trust in the UK government, potentially hindering its AI implementation strategies.

feed.title: Jabil To Set Up Silicon Photonics Unit In Gujarat With $125 Mn Investment
feed.summary: Apple vendor Jabil plans to invest around $125 million (INR 1,000 Cr) to establish a silicon photonics manufacturing factory in Gujarat, India. This unit, which will produce components for high-speed data transmission in industries like autonomous vehicles and 5G, is expected to be operational within the current calendar year. This investment is part of larger commitments in Gujarat's semiconductor sector, totaling INR 15,000 Cr from various domestic and foreign firms.

feed.title: Rs 1,000 cr investment! Apple supplier Jabil to open 2nd facility in THIS state - Details
feed.summary: Apple supplier Jabil plans to invest Rs 1,000 crore to establish its second facility in India, specifically in Gujarat. This new post-wafer fabrication silicon photonics facility aims to strengthen Jabil's presence in India and build on its previous success in Pune. The expansion aligns with customer demand for manufacturing destinations outside of places like Malaysia and is expected to create jobs and foster a domestic semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Apple’s use of SiTime in iPhone 16e fuels stock rally for chipmaker
feed.summary: The inclusion of SiTime components in Apple's new iPhone 16e model has led to a 20% stock rally for the lesser-known chipmaker. SiTime, which produces silicon-based clock components, is seeing this as a significant market opportunity, with Apple reportedly accounting for 22% of its revenue. Apple's newest phone also features its own custom modem chip, the C1, moving away from Qualcomm components.

feed.title: VistaShares Target 15 Berkshire Select Income ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:OMAH)
feed.summary: The VistaShares Target 15 Berkshire Select Income ETF (OMAH) launched on March 5, 2025, and invests in large-cap equities, mostly Berkshire Hathaway positions, using a covered call strategy. The ETF boasts a 14.84% dividend yield and manages assets worth $777.58 million. As of May 22, 2026, OMAH shares have risen 2.1% this year, trading at $19.14.

feed.title: Legislature takes up plan to shield Oregon consumers from data centers’ power costs
feed.summary: Oregon lawmakers are considering House Bill 3546 to protect residential consumers from the rising costs of powering data centers. The bill would classify data centers and cryptocurrency miners as a new utility customer class, giving regulators authority to ensure households and small businesses don't shoulder these costs, and requiring new high-load customers to sign 10-year power purchase contracts. While some tech companies like Google support the idea of multi-year agreements and infrastructure charges, they suggest expanding the bill to cover all large power loads, not just data centers, and including collateral requirements.

feed.title: Outpace Traditional Bond Yields With Calamos ETFs
feed.summary: This article highlights how Calamos ETFs, specifically the Calamos CEF Income & Arbitrage ETF (CCEF) and the Calamos Alternative Nasdaq & Bond ETF (CANQ), can offer investors higher income and better returns than traditional bond strategies. CCEF invests in discounted closed-end funds, providing a 7.20% SEC yield, while CANQ combines fixed income with Nasdaq-100 exposure, yielding 5.14%. Both funds aim to provide diversification and superior performance compared to traditional bond indexes.

feed.title: Truck driver distracted by dating app before crashing into Tomball woman in Bell County in central Texas, lawsuit claims
feed.summary: A lawsuit claims an 18-wheeler driver was distracted by a dating app and his phone shortly before and during a fatal crash in Bell County, Texas, that killed Tracy Rambosek. Evidence from phone records and dashcam video suggests the driver, Barry Giebelstein, was taking screenshots and using a dating app moments before the collision. The lawsuit also alleges that Giebelstein's employer, J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc., had multiple prior warnings about his distracted driving but failed to take adequate action to prevent the crash.

feed.title: Apple defeats AliveCor bid to block US smartwatch imports in US appeal
feed.summary: A U.S. appeals court upheld Apple's win against medical device maker AliveCor in a patent dispute, preventing a potential import ban on Apple Watches. The court affirmed the invalidity of AliveCor's heart-rate monitoring patents that Apple was accused of infringing. This decision negates a U.S. trade tribunal's earlier ruling that Apple had violated AliveCor's rights, though AliveCor plans to explore further legal options.

feed.title: News | Tech CEO follows his customers in relocating to South Florida
feed.summary: Varonis, a technology company, is relocating its headquarters from New York to Miami, following a trend of businesses moving to South Florida. Co-founder and CEO Yaki Faitelson cited Miami's business-friendly environment, quality of life, and ability to attract talent and clients as key factors. The move reflects Miami's growing appeal as a hub for finance, tech, and professional services, though the local office market is currently experiencing a moderation after a pandemic-era boom.

feed.title: Coinbase to Debut Combined Equities & Crypto Index Futures
feed.summary: Coinbase Derivatives is set to launch Mag7 + Crypto Equity Index Futures on September 22, offering the first US-listed derivative that provides combined exposure to traditional equities and cryptocurrency ETFs. This innovative product aims to provide thematic exposure to innovation and growth assets, diversification, and strategic risk management. The index will include the "Magnificent 7" stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, META, TSLA), Coinbase Global Inc. (COIN), and cryptocurrency ETFs (IBIT, ETHA), with an even-weighting methodology and quarterly rebalancing.
